title: Race-heldout validation gate
summary: Current random row validation can overstate performance because laps from the same race/driver/stint are highly dependent.
rationale: Current random row validation can overstate performance because laps from the same race/driver/stint are highly dependent. A group-aware CV score by Race or Year-Race should better match public LB behavior and prevent selecting features that memorize event-specific pit windows.
implementation_hint: Add a lightweight evaluation mode using StratifiedGroupKFold with groups=Race or Year+'_'+Race, preserving class balance where possible. Use it only for model/feature selection, then retrain final model on all train rows.
expected_effect: Lower but more trustworthy CV; may improve public score by rejecting leaky or race-specific features even if random CV drops.
risk: If Kaggle test races overlap heavily with train races, strict heldout-race validation may be too pessimistic and discard useful race identity signals.
